mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates strong consumer demand, which typically translates to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in >55% of observations when RSAFS shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate above a certain threshold signals a weakening labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, thus negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns over a medium horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in >40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is low and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is also low, it indicates an accommodative monetary environment with stable prices, encouraging investment and leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a medium horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative or flat in >50% of periods where both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS are below their respective thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust industrial production, the economy is expanding, indicating strong corporate activity and earnings growth, which typically supports positive S&P 500 returns. This effect is amplified during expansionary phases over a long horizon.
failure_condition: During declared economic expansion periods, the average 60-day forward return when INDPRO grows > 0.2% is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding job market, which underpins consumer confidence and spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a very short horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in >55% of observations when PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) below a critical level signals a significant slowdown in construction and broader economic activity, leading to concerns about future growth and negative S&P 500 returns over a medium horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in >40% of instances where HOUST drops below 1000 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates a tightening monetary policy aimed at curbing overheating, which typically dampens economic activity and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a long horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL > 4.0% AND FEDFUNDS rises > 0.5pp in 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive or flat in >50% of periods where both CPIAUCSL is high and FEDFUNDS is rising significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates a robust and expanding economy, which generally translates into higher corporate profits and investor confidence, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a very short horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in >55% of observations when GDPC1 shows positive quarter-over-quarter growth.
